w:r.w:t: Precision sheet metal ductwork and custom metal fabrication company serving industrial, commercial, and residential projects. Known for their "Better Metal Faster" motto, they specialize in HVAC duct fabrication and custom fittings out of Ephrata, PA.

Brookstone Industries had the craftsmanship and the track record, but their brand and online presence didn’t reflect it. Their existing logo felt dated and failed to communicate the precision and professionalism behind their work. Without a strong visual identity or a website built to convert, they were losing credibility with potential commercial and industrial clients who were evaluating them against more polished competitors. They needed a brand that could hold its own in a highly technical field — one that communicated their “Better Metal Faster” promise at a glance.

Noble developed a complete brand identity for Brookstone, starting with a new logo and visual system built around themes of precision, reliability, and industrial craftsmanship. The brand voice was defined around their core strengths: experience, speed, and quality. Noble then designed and built a full website structured around clear user pathways — service discovery, industry-specific pages, and contact/quote conversion. Testimonials, experience milestones, and service highlights were woven throughout to reinforce trust and authority with decision-makers in commercial and industrial fields. The result is a responsive, professional site that works for field professionals and project managers alike.
(based on Noble's regional presence; sells online at revdtoys.com)

Founded in 2025, REVD Toys creates precision-engineered, scale model farm toys and collectibles for serious collectors and ag enthusiasts. Noble delivered full brand identity, custom retail packaging, and a Shopify website.

REVD Toys was a brand-new company launching in 2025 with a premium product — precision-engineered scale model farm toys — but no brand identity to support it. Entering a niche collectibles market dominated by established names, REVD needed to immediately establish credibility with serious collectors and ag enthusiasts. They also needed packaging designed for retail shelves and an e-commerce website ready to sell from day one, with zero existing assets to build from.

Noble delivered a full brand and digital rollout for REVD Toys from the ground up. This included complete logo development and a cohesive visual identity that reflected the brand’s agricultural roots and commitment to craftsmanship. Custom retail packaging was designed to stand out on shelves while reinforcing the brand’s premium positioning. Noble then built a streamlined Shopify website optimized for clarity, conversion, and growth — giving REVD Toys every touchpoint it needed to launch with confidence and scale.

A 501(c)3 nonprofit founded in 2008 providing affordable sterilization and vaccination services for pets and community cats. Operating three clinic locations across the Lehigh Valley, Reading, and Plains, PA — they've impacted over 190,000 cats and dogs.

No Nonsense Neutering had been operating since 2008 and had grown to three clinic locations across Pennsylvania, impacting over 190,000 cats and dogs. Despite their significant community impact, their digital presence didn’t reflect the scale or credibility of their work. They needed a website that could clearly communicate their services, serve pet owners and rescue organizations across multiple locations, and support their mission of making affordable spay/neuter services accessible throughout the Lehigh Valley, Reading, and Plains.

Noble designed and developed a clean, mission-forward website for No Nonsense Neutering that centered on clarity and accessibility. The site was structured to clearly present their services, clinic locations, and how pet owners and rescue organizations could access affordable sterilization and vaccination services. The design reflects the organization’s no-frills, purpose-driven ethos while making it easy for the community they serve to find what they need and take action.
(referenced in Noble's testimonials and regional client base)

A financial consulting firm built on themes of rebirth and resilience, guiding individuals, entrepreneurs, and organizations through financial challenges toward their next chapter. Noble crafted a full brand identity centered on the symbolism of an ever-burning flame.

Everflame Financial Consulting had a powerful vision — a firm rooted in themes of rebirth, resilience, and forward motion — but no brand to match it. As a new consulting firm entering a competitive landscape, they needed an identity that could communicate both strategic expertise and deeper purpose. The goal was a brand that felt elevated, intentional, and iconic: one that could stand confidently among other financial firms while remaining warm, human-centered, and aspirational.

Noble crafted a full brand identity for Everflame centered on the symbolism of an ever-burning flame — representing resilience, transformation, and the capacity to rise again. Every component was intentionally designed: from the flame icon and custom typography to the color palette and brand messaging. The result is a seamless blend of strategy, storytelling, and symbolism that positions Everflame as an elevated yet deeply human financial consulting brand.

A custom arrow company rooted in trust, integrity, and precision. The brand identity draws on earthy tones, mountain silhouettes, and archer motifs to reflect the rugged, purposeful spirit of the hunt.

Head Strong Flight was launching a custom arrow company with a clear set of values — trust, integrity, and relentless effort — but needed a brand that could communicate those values visually. Operating in the archery and hunting niche, HSF needed to resonate authentically with a discerning audience of serious hunters and outdoor enthusiasts. They required a brand identity that felt both rugged and refined, one that reflected the grit and purpose behind the company without coming across as generic or mass-market.

Noble developed a full brand identity for Head Strong Flight built around the spirit of the hunt. Earthy tones, mountain silhouettes, and archer motifs were combined with bold typography to create a visual system that feels grounded in nature and built for performance. The brand communicates HSF’s core values — precision, integrity, and passion — through every design element, giving the company a confident, distinctive presence in the archery and hunting market.

A Pennsylvania-based company making self-healing polymer shooting targets with a patented color-change feature for instant visual feedback. Serving military, law enforcement, and civilian shooters, Noble manages their social media content and print materials.

DA Targets had an innovative, patented product — self-healing polymer targets with a color-change feature — but their social media and digital presence weren’t reflecting the quality and distinctiveness of what they made. They were struggling to consistently communicate their key differentiators to both professional and recreational shooters across social platforms, and their print materials and website needed ongoing maintenance to stay current as the product line evolved.

Noble took over ongoing social media management, website maintenance, and print materials for DA Targets. Noble developed a consistent content strategy that highlights product demonstrations, key features like the patented color-change technology, user testimonials, and community engagement around firearms training. Print materials were updated and refreshed to match the brand’s standards, and website maintenance ensured the site stayed accurate and functional as the product line grew — including support for new product launches like the HIT IQ Target System.

A beloved community fixture since 1981, Grady's Grill & Truckstop is a roadside diner with deep roots in tradition and local charm. Noble refreshed their brand identity to honor their history while modernizing their visual presence.

Grady’s Grill & Truckstop had been a beloved community institution since 1981, but their visual identity had grown stale over the decades. Their branding no longer matched the warmth and character that made them a regional landmark. They needed a brand refresh that honored their deep roots and history while modernizing their look — one that could help them connect with both longtime regulars and a new generation of customers without losing the charm that defined them.

Noble developed a refreshed brand identity for Grady’s Grill & Truckstop that balanced nostalgia with a modern sensibility. The updated visual system honors the diner’s 40-plus years of tradition while giving the brand a cleaner, more contemporary presence. The result is a brand that feels authentic to Grady’s story — rooted in community and comfort — while looking forward to the next chapter.

A values-driven consulting firm based in York/Lancaster, PA, helping leaders and organizations achieve meaningful growth through servant leadership, integrity, and strategic guidance. Noble developed their full brand identity and website.

VIZION Consulting had a strong set of core values — Integrity, Servant Leadership, Faithfulness, Excellence, Compassion, and Purpose — but lacked the brand and digital infrastructure to communicate them effectively. As a leadership and business consulting firm, they needed a presence that conveyed both credibility and warmth, and a website that could serve as a clear resource for potential clients to understand their philosophy and services.

Noble developed VIZION Consulting’s full brand identity and website, building a visual system that reflects their values-driven, servant leadership approach. The brand communicates trust and purpose without feeling corporate or cold. The website was designed to be user-friendly and informative, clearly presenting VIZION’s services, philosophy, and differentiators — giving potential clients a meaningful first impression and a clear path to engagement.
A community-centered church based in Winter Springs, Florida (near Orlando), focused on authentic connection and gathering. Noble created their brand identity to reflect warmth, belonging, and faith.

The Living Room Church in Winter Springs, Florida was a new congregation centered on authentic connection and gathering, but they had no brand identity to reflect that vision. As a church focused on warmth and belonging, they needed visual branding that felt welcoming and personal — something that communicated the spirit of community and faith without feeling overly formal or institutional.

Noble created The Living Room Church’s brand identity to reflect their core themes of warmth, belonging, and authentic connection. The design language evokes the feeling of a welcoming, lived-in space — approachable and genuine — making visitors feel at home before they ever walk through the door. The visual identity gives the church a consistent, meaningful presence across its website, social media, and community-facing materials.

A new coffeehouse in Strasburg, PA built around community, presence, and connection over a great cup of coffee. Noble created a brand identity blending vintage apothecary charm with modern warmth to reflect the café's inviting spirit.

Wittness Coffeehouse was a brand-new café opening in Strasburg, PA with a clear sense of purpose — community, presence, and connection — but no brand identity to bring it to life. In a competitive coffee market, they needed a visual identity that felt distinctive and intentional, one that could communicate the café’s unique character and invite the community in from the very start.

Noble created a full brand identity for Wittness Coffeehouse that blends vintage apothecary charm with modern warmth. The design balances craft and simplicity, with a visual system that echoes the café’s purpose — a space where people can gather, share stories, and feel grounded. The brand gives Wittness a distinctive, inviting presence that sets the tone for every customer experience from the first glance.

A women's clothing boutique that received a full rebrand to modernize and elevate its look. The refreshed identity features a soft peach and coral palette, clean typography, and a confident message: style should be fun, effortless, and for every woman.

Hey Peaches had an established presence as a women's boutique, but their brand had grown outdated and no longer reflected the fun, confident spirit of the business. Their visual identity needed to be modernized and elevated to better connect with their trend-conscious audience, while still preserving the playful personality that set them apart. The rebrand also needed to strengthen their positioning as a go-to destination for women who want to feel stylish and empowered.

Noble delivered a complete rebrand for Hey Peaches, refreshing the boutique's entire visual and verbal identity. The updated look features clean contemporary typography, a refined palette of soft peaches, warm neutrals, and pops of coral, and more polished layouts — creating a brand that is both elevated and approachable. Lifestyle-driven photography, bold statement graphics, and cohesive packaging reinforce the brand's core message: style should be fun, effortless, and for every woman. The rebrand positions Hey Peaches as a leader in boutique fashion while staying true to its warm, community-driven roots.

A floral design studio in Lexington, Kentucky, rooted in intention and storytelling. Inspired by the Greek word for "world," founder Hannah's brand was built around natural elegance, warm earthy tones, and deeply personal symbolism.

Cosmo Floral Design’s founder Hannah had a story rich with personal symbolism — a name sparked during college, tied to ladybugs, flowers, and serendipitous moments that built a path toward floral design. The challenge was translating that deeply personal journey into a brand that could visually capture the same sense of calm, intention, and full-circle purpose. She needed an identity that felt as thoughtful and meaningful as the arrangements she creates.

Noble worked closely with Hannah to develop a branding system rooted in natural elegance. The logo features custom typography that feels grounded yet refined, paired with a warm earthy color palette to evoke softness and sophistication. Every element — from layout to lettering — was designed to feel poetic and personal, reflecting the founder’s story and her studio’s commitment to intentional, meaningful floral design. The result is a brand that feels like an extension of Hannah herself: graceful, grounded, and deeply thoughtful.

A premier equestrian facility in Lititz, Lancaster County, PA — part of the family-owned Flintrock Corporation. Noble manages their social media, showcasing their riding programs, horse care, and community events.

Flintrock Stables is a premier equestrian facility with a strong reputation built on trust, professionalism, and community, but their social media presence wasn’t keeping pace with their real-world standing. As a hub for riders of all ages and skill levels — from beginners to competitive show participants — they needed a consistent, engaging online presence that could showcase their programs, highlight their team, and grow their connection to the broader equestrian community in Lancaster County.

Noble took over ongoing social media management for Flintrock Stables, developing and executing a content strategy that authentically showcases the facility’s programs, horse care practices, events, and community. By maintaining a consistent and visually engaging presence across platforms, Noble helps Flintrock Stables grow its influence both locally and online — connecting with existing riders, attracting new clients, and reflecting the warmth and professionalism the stables are known for.

A modern lakeside tiny home retreat on Raystown Lake in Pennsylvania, offering elevated comfort surrounded by woods, water, and mountains. Noble handled full brand development, website, and ongoing social media management.

Lakewood Reserve was launching a new short-term rental experience — a modern lakeside tiny home retreat on Raystown Lake — and needed to build an entire brand presence from scratch. In a growing glamping and boutique hospitality market, they needed a brand identity, a website, and social media management that could communicate the elevated, immersive quality of the experience and attract guests looking for something more than a standard vacation rental.

Noble handled full brand development, website design, and ongoing social media management for Lakewood Reserve. The brand was built around the retreat’s core appeal — elevated comfort surrounded by woods, water, and mountains — with a visual identity that feels both modern and grounded in nature. The website and social media presence work together to showcase the experience, build anticipation, and drive bookings, giving Lakewood Reserve a polished, cohesive presence across every touchpoint.
(Noble links to their Instagram as the primary web presence — full site may be in progress)
